If you're looking for a garage door spring replacement company in Livermore, I'd focus on companies with a strong local reputation, experience specifically with torsion spring replacement, and transparent pricing.

Here are the strongest options I found:

### Top Recommendations

  1. Amigo Garage Doors

 * Local Livermore company with many positive customer reviews mentioning broken spring replacement, fast response times, and fair pricing. Customers specifically reported same-day spring replacement service. [Amigo Garage Doors, 346 Mulqueeney St, Livermore, CA 94550, US - MapQuest](https://www.mapquest.com/us/california/amigo-garage-doors-451236242?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

 * If I were choosing a local independent company, this would likely be my first call.

  2. Jarvis Garage Door Service

 * Long-standing East Bay garage door company with numerous reviews referencing spring replacement work, quick service, and reasonable pricing. [Jarvis Garage Door Service, Livermore, CA 94550, US - MapQuest](https://www.mapquest.com/us/california/jarvis-garage-door-service-354240419?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  3. Valiant Garage Door

 * Serves Livermore and advertises same-day spring replacement with stocked spring inventory for common repairs. [Valiant Garage Door | Same-Day Repair, Springs & Openers](https://www.valiantdoor.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### Local Community Favorites

In a Livermore community discussion, residents repeatedly recommended:

  * The Door Doctor

  * Mid-Valley Garage Door

  * Eppler Garage Doors

These were mentioned by multiple local homeowners when asked for garage door repair recommendations. [Recommendation for garage door repair in and around Livermore](https://www.reddit.com/r/Livermore/comments/1ddni4j?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### What You Should Expect to Pay

For a standard residential torsion spring replacement in California, many homeowners report paying roughly $300–$600 for a pair of springs installed, while some larger companies may quote significantly more depending on additional parts and warranties. General industry estimates often place spring replacement around $150–$350 for basic jobs, though Bay Area pricing is usually higher. [How Much Does It Actually Cost to Replace a Garage Door Spring?](https://www.thespruce.com/garage-door-spring-replacement-8635322?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### My Shortlist

If you want the best balance of reputation, responsiveness, and value:

  1. Amigo Garage Doors

  2. Eppler Garage Doors

  3. Mid-Valley Garage Door

Before booking, get at least two quotes and ask:

  * Whether they replace both springs together.

  * The cycle rating of the new springs (10k, 20k, 25k+ cycles).

  * Whether labor and parts are covered by warranty.

  * The total out-the-door price before work begins.

If you'd like, I can also help you compare current Google ratings, warranties, and pricing for these companies.
